session.c(3) Library Functions Manual session.c(3)

NAME

session.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const discovery_config_t *config)
Create a new discovery session. void discovery_session_destroy (discovery_session_t *session)
Destroy discovery session and free resources. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start (discovery_session_t *session)
Start the discovery session. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process (discovery_session_t *session, int64_t timeout_ns)
Process session events (call in main loop). void discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t *session)
Stop the discovery session. discovery_state_t discovery_session_get_state (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get current session state. bool discovery_session_is_active (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if session is active (call in progress). const char * discovery_session_get_string (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get session string. bool discovery_session_is_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the host. session_host_t * discovery_session_get_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Get host context (if we are host). session_participant_t * discovery_session_get_participant (discovery_session_t *session)
Get participant context (if we are participant). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_init_ring (discovery_session_t *session)
Initialize ring consensus state. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round (discovery_session_t *session)
Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new joiner). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive (discovery_session_t *session)
Detect host disconnect (check connection status). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect (discovery_session_t *session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)
Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Become the host (called when elected as future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_connect_to_future_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Connect to pre-elected future host (called when NOT future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session, uint8_t out_id[16], char out_address[64], uint16_t *out_port, uint8_t *out_connection_type)
Get future host information. bool discovery_session_is_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the future host.

Function Documentation

Become the host (called when elected as future host).

Parameters

Session Session context

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success

2371                                                                               {
2372   if (!session) {
2373     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2374     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2375   }
2376 
2377   log_info("Starting as new host after migration (participant ID: %02x%02x...)", session->participant_id[0],
2378            session->participant_id[1]);
2379 
2380   // Get configured port (or use default)
2381   int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
2382 
2383   // Mark ourselves as host
2384   session->is_host = true;
2385 
2386   // Create host context if needed with configured port
2387   if (!session->host_ctx) {
2388     session_host_config_t hconfig = {
2389         .port = host_port,
2390         .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
2391         .max_clients = 32,
2392         .encryption_enabled = true,
2393     };
2394 
2395     session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
2396     if (!session->host_ctx) {
2397       log_error("Failed to create host context for migration");
2398       return ERROR_MEMORY;
2399     }
2400 
2401     // Start listening for new participant connections
2402     asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
2403     if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2404       log_error("Failed to start host after migration: %d", hstart);
2405       return hstart;
2406     }
2407 
2408     log_info("Host restarted after migration, listening on port %d", host_port);
2409   }
2410 
2411   // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so other participants can reconnect
2412   acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
2413   memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2414   memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2415   SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(announcement.host_address));
2416   // TODO: Use actual determined host address instead of 127.0.0.1
2417   announcement.host_port = host_port;
2418   announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
2419   // TODO: Use actual connection type based on NAT quality
2420 
2421   if (session->acds_transport) {
2422     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
2423                               sizeof(announcement), 0);
2424     log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS for new host %02x%02x... at %s:%u", announcement.host_id[0],
2425              announcement.host_id[1], announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
2426   }
2427 
2428   // Mark migration complete
2429   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2430 
2431   // Transition to ACTIVE state (host is ready)
2432   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2433 
2434   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2435 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Detect host disconnect (check connection status).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if host alive, error if disconnected


Definition at line 2248 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2248                                                                                    {
2249   if (!session || session->is_host) {
2250     // We are the host, so "host" is always alive
2251     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2252   }
2253 
2254   // For WebRTC sessions, check the WebRTC transport instead of participant_ctx
2255   if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2256     // If we have a WebRTC transport and it's marked as ready, host is alive
2257     if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
2258       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2259     }
2260     // WebRTC transport not ready yet - might still be connecting
2261     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2262   }
2263 
2264   // For TCP sessions, check participant context
2265   // If we don't have a participant context yet, we're not connected
2266   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2267     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2268   }
2269 
2270   // Check migration state - if we're already migrating, host is definitely not alive
2271   if (session->migration.state != MIGRATION_STATE_NONE) {
2272     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Already detected disconnect
2273   }
2274 
2275   uint64_t now_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2276 
2277   // Check if we have a ping in flight that timed out
2278   if (session->liveness.ping_in_flight) {
2279     uint64_t ping_age_ms = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2280     if (ping_age_ms > session->liveness.timeout_ms) {
2281       // Ping timed out
2282       session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2283       session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
2284       log_warn("Host ping timeout (attempt %u/%u, age=%llu ms)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures,
2285                session->liveness.max_failures, (unsigned long long)ping_age_ms);
2286 
2287       // Check if we've exceeded failure threshold
2288       if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2289         log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive ping failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2290         return ERROR_NETWORK;
2291       }
2292     }
2293   }
2294 
2295   // Check if it's time to send a new ping
2296   uint64_t time_since_last_ping = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2297   if (!session->liveness.ping_in_flight && time_since_last_ping >= session->liveness.ping_interval_ms) {
2298     // Send ping to host via participant connection
2299     socket_t host_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
2300     if (host_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2301       asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(host_socket, PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
2302       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2303         session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = now_ms;
2304         session->liveness.ping_in_flight = true;
2305         log_debug("Sent ping to host (attempt %u/%u)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures + 1,
2306                   session->liveness.max_failures);
2307       } else {
2308         log_warn("Failed to send ping to host: %d", result);
2309         session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2310         if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2311           log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive send failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2312           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2313         }
2314       }
2315     }
2316   }
2317 
2318   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2319 }

2437                                                                                          {
2438   if (!session) {
2439     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2440     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2441   }
2442 
2443   log_info("Reconnecting to future host: %s:%u (connection type: %u)", session->ring.future_host_address,
2444            session->ring.future_host_port, session->ring.future_host_connection_type);
2445 
2446   // Destroy old participant context if present
2447   if (session->participant_ctx) {
2448     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
2449     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
2450   }
2451 
2452   // Create new participant context for the future host
2453   session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
2454       .address = session->ring.future_host_address,
2455       .port = session->ring.future_host_port,
2456       .enable_audio = true,
2457       .enable_video = true,
2458       .encryption_enabled = true,
2459   };
2460 
2461   session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
2462   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2463     log_error("Failed to create participant context for future host");
2464     return ERROR_MEMORY;
2465   }
2466 
2467   // Attempt connection to future host
2468   asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
2469   if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2470     log_error("Failed to connect to future host: %d", pconn);
2471     return pconn;
2472   }
2473 
2474   log_info("Connected to future host after migration");
2475 
2476   // Update our host info with the new future host details
2477   memcpy(session->host_id, session->ring.future_host_id, 16);
2478   SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->ring.future_host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
2479   session->host_port = session->ring.future_host_port;
2480 
2481   log_info("Updated host info to: %s:%u (id: %02x%02x...)", session->host_address, session->host_port,
2482            session->host_id[0], session->host_id[1]);
2483 
2484   // Mark migration complete (in real implementation, this would be done after successful connection)
2485   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2486 
2487   // Transition to ACTIVE state (participant is ready)
2488   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2489 
2490   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2491 }

58   discovery_session_t *session = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(discovery_session_t), discovery_session_t *);

63 
64   session->state = DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT;

79   session->liveness.last_pong_received_ms = 0;
80   session->liveness.consecutive_failures = 0;
81   session->liveness.max_failures = 3;
82   session->liveness.ping_interval_ms = 3 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Ping every 3 seconds
83   session->liveness.timeout_ms = 10 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;      // 10 second timeout
84   session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
85

87            session->host_ctx);
88 
89   // Load or generate identity key
90   char identity_path[256];
91   asciichat_error_t id_result = acds_identity_default_path(identity_path, sizeof(identity_path));
92   if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
93     id_result = acds_identity_load(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
94     if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
95       // File doesn't exist or is corrupted - generate new key
96       log_info("Identity key not found, generating new key");
97       id_result = acds_identity_generate(session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
98       if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
99         // Save for future use
100         acds_identity_save(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
101       }
102     }
103   }
104 
105   if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
106     log_warn("Failed to load/generate identity key, using zero key");
107     memset(session->identity_pubkey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_pubkey));
108     memset(session->identity_seckey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_seckey));
109   } else {
110     char fingerprint[65];
111     acds_identity_fingerprint(session->identity_pubkey, fingerprint);
112     log_info("Using identity key with fingerprint: %.16s...", fingerprint);
113   }
114 
115   // Load multiple identity keys from options (multi-key protocol support)
116   const options_t *opts = options_get();
117   if (opts && opts->num_identity_keys > 0) {
118     // Load all identity keys from options
119     for (size_t i = 0; i < opts->num_identity_keys && i < MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS; i++) {
120       uint8_t pubkey[32] = {0};
121       uint8_t seckey[64] = {0};
122       asciichat_error_t key_result = acds_identity_load(opts->identity_keys[i], pubkey, seckey);
123       if (key_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
124         memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[i], pubkey, 32);
125         char fingerprint[65];
126         acds_identity_fingerprint(pubkey, fingerprint);
127         log_info("Loaded identity key #%zu with fingerprint: %.16s...", i + 1, fingerprint);
128       } else {
129         log_warn("Failed to load identity key #%zu from %s", i + 1, opts->identity_keys[i]);
130       }
131     }
132     session->num_identity_keys = opts->num_identity_keys;
133     log_info("Loaded %zu identity key(s) for multi-key protocol", session->num_identity_keys);
134   } else {
135     // Single-key mode: use only the primary identity key
136     memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[0], session->identity_pubkey, 32);
137     session->num_identity_keys = 1;
138   }
139 
140   // ACDS connection info
141   if (config->acds_url && config->acds_url[0]) {
142     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_url, config->acds_url, sizeof(session->acds_url));
143   }
144   if (config->acds_address && config->acds_address[0]) {
145     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, config->acds_address, sizeof(session->acds_address));
146   } else {
147     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->acds_address));
148   }
149   session->acds_port = config->acds_port > 0 ? config->acds_port : ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT;
150 
151   // Session string (if joining)
152   if (config->session_string && config->session_string[0]) {
153     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->session_string, config->session_string, sizeof(session->session_string));
154     session->is_initiator = false;
155   } else {
156     session->is_initiator = true;
157   }
158 
159   // Callbacks
160   session->on_state_change = config->on_state_change;
161   session->on_session_ready = config->on_session_ready;
162   session->on_error = config->on_error;
163   session->callback_user_data = config->callback_user_data;
164   session->should_exit_callback = config->should_exit_callback;
165   session->exit_callback_data = config->exit_callback_data;
166 
167   log_debug("Discovery session created (initiator=%d, acds=%s:%u)", session->is_initiator, session->acds_address,
168             session->acds_port);
169 
170   return session;
171 }

174   if (!session) {
175     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
176     return;

179   // Close ACDS connection
180   if (session->acds_transport) {
181     acip_transport_destroy(session->acds_transport);

Parameters


Session Session context


disconnect_reason Reason code for disconnect


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on successful failover

2323     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2324     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2325   }
2326 
2327   log_warn("Host disconnect detected: reason=%u", disconnect_reason);
2328 
2329   // Initialize migration context
2330   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED;
2331   session->migration.detection_time_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2332   memcpy(session->migration.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2333   session->migration.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2334 
2335   // Notify ACDS of host loss (lightweight notification, no NAT data exchanged!)
2336   acip_host_lost_t host_lost = {0};
2337   memcpy(host_lost.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2338   memcpy(host_lost.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2339   memcpy(host_lost.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2340   host_lost.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2341   host_lost.disconnect_time_ms = session->migration.detection_time_ms;
2342 
2343   packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_LOST, &host_lost, sizeof(host_lost), 0);
2344 
2345   // Check if we have a pre-elected future host (from last 5-minute ring round)
2346   if (session->ring.future_host_id[0] == 0) {
2347     // No future host known! This shouldn't happen if we just received FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED
2348     // within the last 5 minutes. Fall back to treating this as fatal.
2349     log_error("No future host pre-elected! Session cannot recover from host disconnect.");
2350     session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2351     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Session should end
2352   }
2353 
2354   // **SIMPLIFIED**: No metrics exchange needed! Future host was already elected 5 minutes ago.
2355   // Just failover to the pre-elected host.
2356   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER;
2357 
2358   // Check if I am the future host
2359   if (session->ring.am_future_host) {
2360     // I become the new host immediately! (no election delay!)
2361     log_info("Becoming host (I'm the pre-elected future host)");
2362     return discovery_session_become_host(session);
2363   } else {
2364     // Connect to pre-elected future host (address already stored!)
2365     log_info("Connecting to pre-elected future host: %s:%u", session->ring.future_host_address,
2366              session->ring.future_host_port);
2367     return discovery_session_connect_to_future_host(session);
2368   }
2369 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process
  (discovery_session_t * session, int64_t timeout_ns)


Process session events (call in main loop). Handles incoming ACDS
    messages, negotiation, and state transitions.




Parameters


Session Session context


timeout_ns Max time to wait for events in nanoseconds (0 =
  non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error on failure


Definition at line 1541 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1541                                                                                               {
1542   if (!session) {
1543     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1544   }
1545 
1546   // Handle state-specific processing
1547   switch (session->state) {
1548   case DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER: {
1549     // Wait for PARTICIPANT_JOINED notification from ACDS
1550     // Note: don't use a hard timeout here - let the outer process timeout (snapshot delay) control max wait
1551     // The discovery_run() loop will keep calling this until session becomes active or process exits
1552     uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
1553     uint64_t wait_duration = now_ns - session->waiting_peer_start_ns;
1554 
1555     log_debug("★ WAITING_PEER: duration=%.3fs (no timeout, waiting for peer...)", wait_duration / 1e9);
1556 
1557     if (session->acds_transport && timeout_ns > 0) {
1558       // Do not set socket timeout - let receive_packet_secure use its own timeout
1559       // Setting a short socket timeout conflicts with its hardcoded 1-second timeout
1560       packet_type_t type;
1561       void *data = NULL;
1562       size_t len = 0;
1563       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1564 
1565       // Try to receive a packet
1566       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1567           packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1568       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1569         if (type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED) {
1570           log_info("Peer joined session, transitioning to negotiation");
1571           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING);
1572           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1573         } else {
1574           // Got some other packet, handle it or ignore
1575           log_debug("Received packet type %d while waiting for peer", type);
1576           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1577         }
1578       } else if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1579         // Timeout is normal, just return
1580       } else {
1581         log_debug("Packet receive error while waiting for peer: %d", recv_result);
1582       }
1583     } else {
1584       // Fallback to sleep if no timeout or invalid transport
1585       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1586     }
1587     break;
1588   }
1589 
1590   case DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING: {
1591     // Handle NAT negotiation
1592     if (session->negotiate.state == NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT) {
1593       // Initialize negotiation
1594       negotiate_init(&session->negotiate, session->session_id, session->participant_id, session->is_initiator);
1595       log_info("Starting NAT negotiation (initiator=%d)", session->is_initiator);
1596 
1597       // Send our NETWORK_QUALITY to ACDS so peers can evaluate us
1598       asciichat_error_t send_result = send_network_quality_to_acds(session);
1599       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1600         log_warn("Failed to send initial NETWORK_QUALITY: %d", send_result);
1601       }
1602 
1603       // ACDS relays NETWORK_QUALITY only to OTHER participants, not back to sender
1604       // For now in discovery mode, assume we're the only one and become host immediately
1605       // A real implementation would wait for peer to join via PARTICIPANT_JOINED, then connect directly
1606       log_info("Proceeding as host (ACDS cannot relay peer quality in solo mode)");
1607       session->negotiate.our_quality.detection_complete = true;
1608       session->negotiate.peer_quality_received = true;
1609       memset(&session->negotiate.peer_quality, 0, sizeof(session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1610       // Mark peer quality as detected but with worst NAT type so we win host election
1611       session->negotiate.peer_quality.detection_complete = true;
1612       session->negotiate.peer_quality.nat_type = ACIP_NAT_TYPE_SYMMETRIC;
1613     }
1614 
1615     // Determine host role (now that peer_quality is set)
1616     if (session->negotiate.peer_quality_received && session->negotiate.state != NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING) {
1617       log_info("Determining host role based on NAT qualities...");
1618       asciichat_error_t result = negotiate_determine_result(&session->negotiate);
1619       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1620         session->is_host = session->negotiate.we_are_host;
1621 
1622         if (session->is_host) {
1623           // We become the host
1624           log_info("🏠 NAT negotiation complete: WE ARE HOST (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1625                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1626                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1627           memcpy(session->host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1628           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1629           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1630           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST);
1631         } else {
1632           // They are host (shouldn't happen in solo mode, but handle it)
1633           log_info("👤 NAT negotiation complete: THEY ARE HOST at %s:%u (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1634                    session->negotiate.host_address, session->negotiate.host_port,
1635                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1636                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1637           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1638           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1639           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST);
1640         }
1641       } else {
1642         log_error("Failed to determine negotiation result: %d", result);
1643         set_error(session, result, "Host election failed");
1644       }
1645     }
1646     break;
1647   }
1648 
1649   case DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST: {
1650     // Start hosting
1651     if (!session->host_ctx) {
1652       // Create host context with configured port
1653       int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1654 
1655       session_host_config_t hconfig = {
1656           .port = host_port,
1657           .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
1658           .max_clients = 32,
1659           .encryption_enabled = true,
1660       };
1661 
1662       session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
1663       if (!session->host_ctx) {
1664         log_error("Failed to create host context");
1665         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create host context");
1666         break;
1667       }
1668 
1669       // Start listening
1670       asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
1671       if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1672         log_error("Failed to start host: %d", hstart);
1673         set_error(session, hstart, "Failed to start host");
1674         break;
1675       }
1676 
1677       log_info("Host started, listening for connections");
1678     }
1679 
1680     // Notify listeners that we're ready
1681     if (session->on_session_ready) {
1682       session->on_session_ready(session->session_string, session->callback_user_data);
1683     }
1684 
1685     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1686     break;
1687   }
1688 
1689   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST: {
1690     // Connect to host as participant
1691 
1692     // For WebRTC sessions, wait for DataChannel to open
1693     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
1694       // Check if transport is ready (DataChannel opened)
1695       if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
1696         log_info("WebRTC DataChannel established, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1697         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1698         break;
1699       }
1700 
1701       // If not ready and we haven't initiated connection yet, initiate it
1702       if (session->peer_manager && !session->webrtc_connection_initiated) {
1703         log_info("Initiating WebRTC connection to host (attempt %d/%d)...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1704                  GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts) + 1);
1705 
1706         // Initiate connection (generates offer, triggers SDP exchange)
1707         asciichat_error_t conn_result =
1708             webrtc_peer_manager_connect(session->peer_manager, session->session_id, session->host_id);
1709 
1710         if (conn_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1711           log_error("Failed to initiate WebRTC connection: %d", conn_result);
1712           set_error(session, conn_result, "Failed to initiate WebRTC connection");
1713           break;
1714         }
1715 
1716         log_info("WebRTC connection initiated, waiting for DataChannel...");
1717         session->webrtc_connection_initiated = true;
1718         session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() / 1000;
1719       }
1720 
1721       // Check for ICE gathering timeout on all peers
1722       if (session->webrtc_connection_initiated && session->peer_manager) {
1723         int timeout_ms = GET_OPTION(webrtc_ice_timeout_ms);
1724         int timed_out_count = webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts(session->peer_manager, timeout_ms);
1725 
1726         if (timed_out_count > 0) {
1727           log_error("ICE gathering timeout: %d peer(s) failed to gather candidates within %dms", timed_out_count,
1728                     timeout_ms);
1729 
1730           // Check if we have retries remaining
1731           int max_attempts = GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts);
1732           if (session->webrtc_retry_attempt < max_attempts) {
1733             // Calculate exponential backoff delay
1734             uint32_t backoff_ms = calculate_backoff_delay_ms(session->webrtc_retry_attempt);
1735 
1736             log_warn("WebRTC connection attempt %d/%d failed, retrying in %ums...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1737                      max_attempts + 1, backoff_ms);
1738 
1739             // Wait for backoff period
1740             platform_sleep_ms(backoff_ms);
1741 
1742             // Destroy old peer manager
1743             webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
1744             session->peer_manager = NULL;
1745 
1746             // Re-initialize peer manager for retry
1747             asciichat_error_t reinit_result = initialize_webrtc_peer_manager(session);
1748             if (reinit_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1749               log_error("Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry: %d", reinit_result);
1750               set_error(session, reinit_result, "Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry");
1751 
1752               // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error
1753               if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1754                 log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1755                 set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1756                 return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1757               }
1758 
1759               break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1760             }
1761 
1762             // Reset connection state for retry
1763             session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
1764             session->webrtc_retry_attempt++;
1765 
1766             log_info("WebRTC peer manager re-initialized for attempt %d/%d", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1767                      max_attempts + 1);
1768           } else {
1769             // No more retries - connection failed
1770             log_error("WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (timeout: %dms)", max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1771 
1772             // Set error state - WebRTC connection failed
1773             char error_msg[256];
1774             snprintf(error_msg, sizeof(error_msg), "WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (%dms timeout)",
1775                      max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1776             set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, error_msg);
1777 
1778             // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error (no TCP fallback)
1779             if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1780               log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1781               set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1782               return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1783             }
1784 
1785             break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1786           }
1787         }
1788       }
1789 
1790       // Poll ACDS for WebRTC signaling messages (SDP answer, ICE candidates)
1791       // while waiting for DataChannel to open
1792       if (session->acds_transport) {
1793         // Check if data available (non-blocking with timeout_ns)
1794         struct timeval tv_timeout;
1795         tv_timeout.tv_sec = timeout_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
1796         tv_timeout.tv_usec = (timeout_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT) / 1000;
1797 
1798         fd_set readfds;
1799         FD_ZERO(&readfds);
1800         FD_SET(session->acds_socket, &readfds);
1801 
1802         int select_result = select(session->acds_socket + 1, &readfds, NULL, NULL, &tv_timeout);
1803         if (select_result > 0 && FD_ISSET(session->acds_socket, &readfds)) {
1804           // Data available to read
1805           packet_type_t type;
1806           void *data = NULL;
1807           size_t len = 0;
1808           void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1809 
1810           asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1811               packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1812 
1813           if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1814             // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1815             handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1816             buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1817           } else {
1818             log_debug("Failed to receive ACDS packet: %d", recv_result);
1819           }
1820         }
1821       }
1822 
1823       // Stay in CONNECTING_HOST until DataChannel opens (webrtc_transport_ready becomes true)
1824       // The discovery_on_transport_ready callback will set webrtc_transport_ready=true
1825       break;
1826     }
1827 
1828     if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1829       // Create participant context for this session
1830       session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
1831           .address = session->host_address,
1832           .port = session->host_port,
1833           .enable_audio = true,
1834           .enable_video = true,
1835           .encryption_enabled = true,
1836       };
1837 
1838       session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
1839       if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1840         log_error("Failed to create participant context");
1841         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create participant context");
1842         break;
1843       }
1844 
1845       // Attempt connection
1846       asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
1847       if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1848         log_error("Failed to connect as participant: %d", pconn);
1849         // Stay in this state, will retry on next process() call
1850         break;
1851       }
1852 
1853       log_info("Connected to host, performing crypto handshake...");
1854 
1855       // Perform crypto handshake with server
1856       // Get the socket from participant context
1857       socket_t participant_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
1858       if (participant_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1859         log_error("Failed to get socket from participant context");
1860         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to get participant socket");
1861         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1862         break;
1863       }
1864 
1865       // Step 1: Send client protocol version
1866       log_debug("Sending protocol version to server...");
1867       protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1868       client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
1869       client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
1870       client_version.supports_encryption = 1;                // We support encryption
1871       client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1872       client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1873       client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1874 
1875       int result = send_protocol_version_packet(participant_socket, &client_version);
1876       if (result != 0) {
1877         log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
1878         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send protocol version");
1879         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1880         break;
1881       }
1882 
1883       // Step 2: Receive server protocol version
1884       log_debug("Receiving server protocol version...");
1885       packet_type_t packet_type;
1886       void *payload = NULL;
1887       size_t payload_len = 0;
1888 
1889       int recv_result = receive_packet(participant_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
1890       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1891         log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type 0x%x)", packet_type);
1892         if (payload) {
1893           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1894         }
1895         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive protocol version from server");
1896         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1897         break;
1898       }
1899 
1900       if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
1901         log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu", payload_len);
1902         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1903         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid protocol version packet");
1904         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1905         break;
1906       }
1907 
1908       protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
1909       memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
1910       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1911 
1912       if (!server_version.supports_encryption) {
1913         log_error("Server does not support encryption");
1914         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Server does not support encryption");
1915         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1916         break;
1917       }
1918 
1919       log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (encryption: yes)", NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version),
1920                NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision));
1921 
1922       // Step 3: Send crypto capabilities
1923       log_debug("Sending crypto capabilities...");
1924       crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
1925       client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001);    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1926       client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0003);   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE
1927       client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001); // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1928       client_caps.requires_verification = 0;
1929       client_caps.preferred_kex = 0x0001;    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1930       client_caps.preferred_auth = 0x0001;   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519
1931       client_caps.preferred_cipher = 0x0001; // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1932 
1933       result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(participant_socket, &client_caps);
1934       if (result != 0) {
1935         log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1936         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1937         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1938         break;
1939       }
1940 
1941       log_info("Crypto handshake initiated successfully");
1942       log_warn("*** Connected to host as participant - transitioning to ACTIVE ***");
1943     }
1944 
1945     // Check if connection is established
1946     bool ctx_valid = session->participant_ctx != NULL;
1947     bool is_connected = ctx_valid ? session_participant_is_connected(session->participant_ctx) : false;
1948     log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - participant_ctx=%p, ctx_valid=%d, is_connected=%d",
1949               session->participant_ctx, ctx_valid, is_connected);
1950 
1951     if (ctx_valid && !is_connected) {
1952       // Log more details about why it's not connected
1953       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - context exists but not connected yet");
1954     }
1955 
1956     if (is_connected) {
1957       log_info("Participant connection confirmed, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1958       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1959     } else {
1960       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - awaiting connection establishment (ctx_valid=%d, "
1961                 "is_connected=%d)",
1962                 ctx_valid, is_connected);
1963     }
1964     break;
1965   }
1966 
1967   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE:
1968     // Receive and handle ACDS packets for WebRTC signaling (NEW)
1969     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && session->acds_transport) {
1970       // Use non-blocking polling to check for incoming ACDS packets
1971       struct pollfd pfd = {.fd = session->acds_socket, .events = POLLIN, .revents = 0};
1972 
1973       int poll_result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, 0); // Non-blocking poll (timeout=0)
1974 
1975       if (poll_result > 0 && (pfd.revents & POLLIN)) {
1976         // Data available to read
1977         packet_type_t type;
1978         void *data = NULL;
1979         size_t len = 0;
1980         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1981 
1982         asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1983             packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1984 
1985         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1986           // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1987           handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1988           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1989         }
1990       }
1991     }
1992 
1993     // Session is active - check for host disconnect
1994     if (!session->is_host) {
1995       // We are a participant - check if host is still alive
1996       asciichat_error_t host_check = discovery_session_check_host_alive(session);
1997       if (host_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1998         // Host is down! Trigger automatic failover to pre-elected future host
1999         log_warn("Host disconnect detected during ACTIVE state");
2000 
2001         // If --prefer-webrtc is set, WebRTC failure is fatal (no TCP fallback)
2002         const options_t *opts = options_get();
2003         if (opts && opts->prefer_webrtc) {
2004           log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
2005           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
2006           session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
2007           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2008         }
2009 
2010         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING);
2011         // Reason code: 1 = timeout/disconnect detected during ACTIVE session
2012         discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect(session, 1);
2013       }
2014     }
2015     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
2016     break;
2017 
2018   case DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING:
2019     // Host migration in progress
2020     // Check if migration has timed out (if it takes >30 seconds, give up)
2021     if (session->migration.state == MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE) {
2022       // Migration completed successfully
2023       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2024       log_info("Migration complete, session resumed");
2025     } else if (session_get_current_time_ms() - session->migration.detection_time_ms > 30 * MS_PER_SEC_INT) {
2026       // Migration timed out after 30 seconds
2027       log_error("Host migration timeout - session cannot recover");
2028       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
2029       session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
2030     }
2031     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
2032     break;
2033 
2034   default:
2035     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid session state");
2036     break;
2037   }
2038 
2039   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2040 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start the discovery session. Connects to ACDS and either creates
    or joins a session.




Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 1344 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1344                                                                         {
1345   log_info("discovery_session_start: ENTRY - session=%p", session);
1346 
1347   if (!session) {
1348     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1349   }
1350 
1351   log_info("discovery_session_start: is_initiator=%d, session_string='%s'", session->is_initiator,
1352            session->session_string);
1353 
1354   // Check if we should exit before starting blocking operations
1355   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1356     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (before ACDS connect)");
1357     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1358   }
1359 
1360   log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling connect_to_acds...");
1361   // Connect to ACDS
1362   asciichat_error_t result = connect_to_acds(session);
1363   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1364     log_error("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds FAILED - result=%d", result);
1365     return result;
1366   }
1367   log_info("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds succeeded");
1368 
1369   // Check again after connection
1370   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1371     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after ACDS connect)");
1372     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1373   }
1374 
1375   // Perform crypto handshake with ACDS (same as client-server handshake)
1376   log_info("discovery_session_start: Performing crypto handshake with ACDS...");
1377   crypto_handshake_context_t handshake_ctx;
1378   memset(&handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(handshake_ctx));
1379 
1380   result = crypto_handshake_init("discovery_client", &handshake_ctx, false);
1381   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1382     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake init failed - %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1383     set_error(session, result, "Crypto handshake init failed");
1384     return result;
1385   }
1386 
1387   // Store identity key in handshake context
1388   handshake_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1389   handshake_ctx.client_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1390   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_public_key.key, session->identity_pubkey, 32);
1391   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_private_key.key.ed25519, session->identity_seckey, 64);
1392 
1393   // Step 0: Receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS from ACDS
1394   {
1395     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1396     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1397     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1398     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1399 
1400     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1401     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
1402       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS (got type %u)", pkt_type);
1403       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1404       set_error(session, result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
1405                 "Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS");
1406       return result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1407     }
1408 
1409     if (pkt_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
1410       log_error("discovery_session_start: Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size: %zu (expected %zu)", pkt_len,
1411                 sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1412       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1413       set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size");
1414       return ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1415     }
1416 
1417     crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
1418     memcpy(&server_params, pkt_data, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1419     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1420 
1421     // Pass params in network byte order — crypto_handshake_set_parameters
1422     // handles conversion internally for client (is_server=false)
1423     result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&handshake_ctx, &server_params);
1424     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1425       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to set crypto parameters");
1426       set_error(session, result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
1427       return result;
1428     }
1429     log_info("discovery_session_start: CRYPTO_PARAMETERS received and applied");
1430   }
1431 
1432   // Step 1: Key exchange - receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT and respond
1433   {
1434     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1435     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1436     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1437     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1438 
1439     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1440     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1441       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT");
1442       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange recv failed");
1443       return result;
1444     }
1445 
1446     result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1447     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1448 
1449     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1450       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake key exchange failed");
1451       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange failed");
1452       return result;
1453     }
1454   }
1455 
1456   // Step 2: Auth response - receive AUTH_CHALLENGE and respond
1457   {
1458     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1459     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1460     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1461     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1462 
1463     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1464     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1465       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive AUTH_CHALLENGE");
1466       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response recv failed");
1467       return result;
1468     }
1469 
1470     result =
1471         crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1472     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1473 
1474     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1475       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake auth response failed");
1476       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response failed");
1477       return result;
1478     }
1479   }
1480 
1481   // Step 3: Complete handshake
1482   // Note: Some servers (like ACDS without auth) complete the handshake in step 2,
1483   // so check if we're already done before trying to receive another packet
1484   if (handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1485     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1486     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1487     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1488     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1489 
1490     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1491     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1492       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE");
1493       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete recv failed");
1494       return result;
1495     }
1496 
1497     result = crypto_handshake_client_complete(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1498     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1499 
1500     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1501       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake complete failed");
1502       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete failed");
1503       return result;
1504     }
1505   } else {
1506     log_info("discovery_session_start: Handshake already complete from auth_response step");
1507   }
1508 
1509   log_info("discovery_session_start: Crypto handshake with ACDS completed");
1510 
1511   // Check again after handshake
1512   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1513     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after handshake)");
1514     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1515   }
1516 
1517   // Create or join session
1518   if (session->is_initiator) {
1519     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling create_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1520     result = create_session(session);
1521     log_info("discovery_session_start: create_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, result=%d",
1522              session->participant_ctx, result);
1523 
1524     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1525       // Initiator also needs to register itself as a participant so it can receive PARTICIPANT_JOINED notifications
1526       // when other peers join. This makes the initiator discoverable to other participants.
1527       log_info("Initiator: now registering self as participant in created session");
1528       result = join_session(session);
1529       log_info("Initiator: join_session result=%d", result);
1530     }
1531     return result;
1532   } else {
1533     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling join_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1534     asciichat_error_t result = join_session(session);
1535     log_info("discovery_session_start: join_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, state=%d, result=%d",
1536              session->participant_ctx, session->state, result);
1537     return result;
1538   }
1539 }
